sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.28 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a group. A function$f\! : G { C}$is called normed\/ if$f(1)=1$; central\/ if$f(gh)=f(hg)$for all$g,h G$; positive-definite\/ if$_{k,\, l}f(g_k^{-1}g_l)\, _kc_l 0$for any$g_1, ,g_n G$and any -3ex$c_1, ,c_n { C}$. Classify the infinite simple locally finite groups$G$which possess functions satisfying 1)--3). The simple Chevalley groups are known to have no such functions, while such functio...
